title: Elongated medical device suitable for intravascular insertion optical force sensing assembly for an elongated medical device and method of making a medical optical force sensing assembly
abstract: The present invention concerns an elongated medical device (1) suitable for intravascular insertion. Said device comprising a flexible elongated body (2) having a distal portion (3) with a distal end (4) and a proximal portion (5) and an optic force sensing assembly (20) disposed within said flexible elongated body (2) proximate said distal end. The optical force sensing assembly (20) comprises a light source (30), which defines a linear optical light source axis (A), and an optical sensor (40), which faces the light source (30) and which defines a linear optical sensor axis (B) and whereat the optical sensor (40) is arranged in a distance (do, di) to the light source along the optical sensor axis (B), a mounting assembly (50) for the optical sensor (40) and the light source (30) which allows for relative movement of the light source (30) against the optical sensor (40) at least in the directions X, Y, Z of the Cartesian coordinate system, wherein direction Z is parallel to the direction of the optical sensor axis (B) at least in an initial state of the optical force sensing assembly (20) and whereby X and Y directions are perpendicular to each other as well as perpendicular to Z direction.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . ) An elongated medical device (1 ) suitable for intravascular insertion, said device compris- ing a flexible elongated body (2) having a distal portion (3) with a distal end (4) and a proximal portion (5) and an optic force sensing assembly (20) disposed within said flexible elongated body (2) proximate said distal end, characterized in that, said optical force sensing assembly (20) comprises: a light source (30), which defines a linear optical light source axis (A), and an optical sensor (40), which faces the light source (30) and which defines a linear optical sensor axis (B) and whereby the optical sensor (40) is arranged in a distance (d 0 , di) to the light source along the optical sensor axis (B), a mounting assembly (50) for the optical sensor (40) and the light source (30) which allows for relative movement of the light source (30) against the optical sensor (40) at least in the directions X, Y, Z of a Cartesian coordinate system, whereby direction Z is parallel to the direction of the optical sensor axis (B) at least in an initial state of the optical force sensing assembly (20) and whereby X and Y directions are perpendicular to each other as well as perpendicular to Z direction. 2. ) Medical device (1 ) according to claim 1 , characterized in that, the optical sensor (40) and the light source (30) are both disposed at the distal portion (3) of the elongated body (2). 3. ) Medical device (1 ) according to claim 1 or 2, characterized in that, the distance (d 0 ) has a maximum length of 1 0 mm, preferably 1 mm, even more preferably 0.5 mm. 4. ) Medical device (1 ) according to any one of claims 1 to 3, characterized in that, the optical sensor (40) is a camera module, preferably a waver-level camera. 5. ) Medical device (1 ) according to any one of claims 1 to 4, characterized in that, the camera module is smaller than 1 .5 mm x 1 .5 mm in its width and/or smaller than 1 .5 mm in its diameter, whereby each of width and diameter is in a direction perpendicular to the optical sensor axis (B). 6.) Medical device (1 ) according to any one of claims 1 to 5, characterized in that, the camera module has a view angle a in air between 60 and 1 80 degrees, preferentially between 90 and 120 degrees. 7.) Medical device (1 ) according to any one of claims 1 to 6,

Medical device (1 ) according to any one of claims 1 1 to 1 3, characterized in that, the elastic element (51 , 55) is made at least partially of a rubber-like material. 1 5.) Medical device (1 ) according to any one of claims 1 1 to 14, characterized in that the light source mounting (53) is rigidly connected to the distal end (4) of the elongated body (2). 1 6.) Medical device (1 ) according to any one of claims 1 1 to 1 5, characterized in that 

 the mounting assembly (50) is adapted to movably connect the l ight source mounti ng to the optical sensor mounting (54) in a way that the optical light source axis (A) and the optical sensor axis (B) always run paral lel to each other. ) Medical device (1 ) according to any one of claims 4 to 1 6 characterized i n that the camera module has a pixel array (41 ) of n pixels (42) whereby in operation a contiguous array of pixels (47) is receiving l ight from the light source (30), whereby the size of the i l luminated contiguous pixel array (43) is proportional to a force (F) applied to the distal end in the Z direction. Medical device (1 ) according to claim 1 7, wherein a deviation in shape of the illuminated contiguous pixel array (43) is a measure for a force (F) appl ied in the X and /or Y directions or any combi nation of these. Medical device (1 ) accordi ng to claim 1 7, characterized i n that the force (F) appl ied to the distal end in the Z direction may be calculated by the formula F=k * d 0 *(1 -io/i,) whereby k is the spri ng constant i n N/mm, do is the distance in mm of the optical sensor (40) to the light source (40) in the initial position of the l ight source (30) in respect to the optical sensor (40) when no force is appl ied to the distal end (4) of the elongated body, 

 is a measure of the size of the illuminated contiguous pixel array (43) that is receiving light from the light source (30) in the initial position of the light source (40) in respect to the optical sensor (40) when no force is applied to the distal end (4) of the elongated body (2), and 11 is a measure of the size of the illuminated contiguous pixel array (43) that is receiving light from the light source (30) in a position of the light source (30) in respect to the optical sensor (40) when the force (F) is applied to the distal end (4) of the elongated body (2). Medical device (1 ) according to any one of claims 4 to 1 9, characterized in that in operation, a center (C) of the illuminated contiguous pixel array (43) is coaxial with the optical sensor axis (B) when there is no force applied to the distal end (2) in the X or Y directions and that in operation, the center (C) of the illuminated contiguous pixel array (43) is non-coaxial to the optical sensor axis (B) when there is a force (F) applied to the distal end in the X or Y directions. Medical device (1 ) according to any one of claims 1 to 20, characterized in that it comprises a data processing and control unit (1 5) that is set up to process force sensing data delivered from the optical sensor (40) via a data line (12) and to output force data by means of a data output unit (1 6). Medical device (1 ) according to any one of claims 1 to 21 , 

 characterized in that it is formed as a catheter for exploration or treatment of a vessel, organ or other body cavity. 23.) Medical optical force sensing assembly according to claim 26, characterized in that, the distance (do) has a maximum length of 1 0 mm, preferably 1 mm, even more preferably 0.5 mm. 30.) the spring constant in N/mm, do is the distance (d) in mm of the optical sensor (40) to the light source (30) in the initial position of the light source (30) in respect to the optical sensor (40) when no force is applied to any one of the light source mounting (53) and the optical sensor mounting (54). 10 is a measure of the size of the i lluminated contiguous pixel array (43) that is when there is a force (F) applied to any one of the light source mounting (53) and the optical sensor mounting (54) in the X or Y directions. 44. ) Medical optical force sensing assembly (20) according to any one of claims 26 and 29 to 43, characterized in that it is adapted to be connected to a data processing and control unit (1 5) that is set up to process force sensing data delivered from the optical sensor (40) via a data line (12) and to output force data by means of a data output unit (1 6). 45.) Method of making a Medical optical force sensing assembly (20), comprising: providing a mounting assembly (50) that comprises a light source mounting (53) for a light source (30) and an optical sensor mounting (54) for an optical sensor (40), mount the light source (30) on the light source mounting (53) and the optical sensor (40) on the optical sensor mounting (54), so that the light source (30) faces the optical sensor, arrange an elastic element (51 , 52, 55) between the light source mounting (53) and the optical sensor mounting (54) to movably connect the light source mounting (53) and the optical sensor mounting (54) and to allow for a relative movement of the light source (30) against the optical sensor (40) at least in the directions X, Y, Z of a Cartesian coordinate system. 

 The method of claim 45, wherein the optical sensor (40) defines a linear optical sensor axis (B) and wherein the optical sensor is arranged in a distance (do, di) to the light source along its optical axis (B), whereby direction Z is parallel to the direction of the optical sensor axis (B) at least in an initial state of the medical optical force sensing assembly (20) and whereby X and Y directions are perpendicular to each other as well as perpendicular to Z direction.
